IA4S-023,"How to create a tracking table","health,tracking","Scheduled_Jobs","Line 1: I am only retrieving 1-day worth of data not 30 days after I am done filtering Lines 2-5: I am using a transforming command so we can batch process the NEW data faster and calculating my earliest and my latest events in the dataset.Line 6: I am appending the table, so I don’t lose any older data Lines 7-10: I am using a transforming command so we can batch process ALL NEW & OLD data faster and calculating my earliest and my latest events in the dataset.Line 11: I am comparing against relative time, think of this as maintaining 30 days’ worth of data, if you need 1 day just use -1d, if you need 8 hours use -8h etc.Line 12: Output the lookup and make sure we don’t create an empty table by accident Line 13: Finish with stats count","index = ... sourcetype = ... field = ... earliest=-24h@h latest=+0s

column1: IA4S-100,"Lists Search Head Cluster artifacts and replicas","artifacts,onprem","Search_Head_Cluster","Provides list of artifacts and replicas currently managed by the captain across a searchhead cluster.
This endpoint can only be accessed on the captain. The response lists all artifacts that are currently resident on the set of search head cluster members.
An artifact in search head clustering is a managed search directory. Currently, only scheduled search results directories are managed and replicated according to replication policy.
Note: Ad hoc searches are not considered artifacts and are not listed.
origin_guid: Guid of the origin peer where this artifact was created/search was run.
peers: Lists information about replicas of this artifact on members of this searchhead cluster.
service_after_time: Artifact service/fixup is deferred until after this time.
remote_sids: Required. Set this to true to return the searches that the captain is seeing. Will include adhoc searches on remote members.","

column2: fields splunk_server app user title label service_after_time artifact_size",

column1: IA4S-228,"msearch command examples",examples,Commands,"The msearch command is used to return raw data-points in a metrics index.

It is NOT designed to work at scale since returning every metric event over a huge data set is prohibitively expensive.  Having said that, there are many use cases and workflows where seeing raw data points in the metrics index can be helpful, mostly around GDI/data onboarding/data discovery use cases.
The filter predicate can contain predicates involving dimensions or metric_name ONLY.
To constrain _time, one has to do it via API (TimePicker or specifying -[earliest/latest] as argument in CLI)
The index/es must be metrics indexes.  Only Quake version buckets are processed,
old buckets that are pre-Quake will be silently ignored by command.

column1: IA4S-270,"Data Hygiene events future","health,scma",Health,"This check looks for events that are with a future timestamp.
failure_text: One or more sourcetypes has been found to present events in the future.

suggested_action: It is recommended to review identified sourcetypes for data onboarding issues (incorrectly parsing time, not considering timezone, etc) or source configuration issues (such as NTP settings, invalid timezone, etc). Events that are in the future are generally not considered part of search process due to fact they are outside search window, resulting in misleading or inaccurate reporting. Work with Splunk Professional Services to determine next steps.
title: Indexing - Data Hygiene Events in the future","
column2: tstats summariesonly=t count AS event_count dc(source) AS source_count dc(host) AS host_count latest(_time) AS latest_event_time where index=* AND (earliest=+5m latest=+10y) by index, sourcetype
eval severity_level=3, message=""Event is in future by "".tostring(latest_event_time-now(), ""duration"")

column4: stats values(severity_level) AS severity_level values(*_count) AS *_count]

The first line gets a list of all jobs running on all your stack, you can filter by shcluster_label, app, provenance etc…

Then I extract all macros that are used in the search string using the Insights App for Splunk Macro get_macro_reference and after I retrieve the Macros used which will be in the form of MACRO_NAME or MACRO_NAME(1) etc…, I use another Insights App for Splunk Macro
